title: Axel's Big Feelings at Bedtime
synopsis: When bedtime feels scary, little Axel learns that talking about his feelings and having a cozy routine can help him feel safe and happy. A gentle story about managing bedtime emotions.

pages.text: Axel felt scared when Mama said it was bedtime. His tummy felt funny and his heart beat fast. "I don't want to go to bed," he said with a frown.

illustration.description: A young Black boy with curly hair wearing blue pajamas, looking worried with wide eyes and a small frown. He's standing in a bright living room with yellow walls and colorful toys scattered around. His posture shows he's feeling anxious about bedtime.
illustration.alt: Axel looking scared about bedtime in his bright living room

pages.text: "What makes you feel scared?" asked Mama gently. Axel pointed to his dark bedroom. "The shadows look big and scary," he whispered.

illustration.description: A warm scene showing Axel pointing toward his bedroom doorway while his mama, a Black woman with natural hair wearing a green sweater, kneels down to his level with a caring expression. The bedroom doorway shows gentle shadows, and the hallway is lit with warm orange light.
illustration.alt: Axel talking to his mama about what scares him at bedtime

pages.text: Mama hugged Axel tight. "It's okay to feel scared sometimes," she said. "Let's make bedtime feel safe and cozy together." Axel felt a little better in Mama's warm hug.

illustration.description: A tender moment showing Mama giving Axel a comforting hug in the hallway. Both have peaceful expressions, and the lighting is warm and golden. Axel's face shows he's starting to feel more secure, with his worried expression softening.
illustration.alt: Mama hugging Axel to help him feel better

pages.text: They turned on Axel's special night light that made star shapes on the wall. "Look!" said Axel, feeling a bit brave. "The scary shadows are gone!"

illustration.description: Axel's bedroom transformed with a warm night light casting cheerful star patterns on the walls. Axel points excitedly at the stars with a smile beginning to form. The room feels cozy with soft purple and blue tones, and his bed has a bright red comforter.
illustration.alt: Axel's room with magical star night light making him feel brave

pages.text: Mama tucked Axel into his soft bed and read his favorite story about a brave little bear. Axel felt happy and safe now, snuggled under his cozy blanket.

illustration.description: A peaceful bedtime scene with Mama sitting on the edge of Axel's bed reading a colorful picture book. Axel is tucked in with his red blanket, looking content and sleepy. The room glows softly from the star night light, creating a magical, safe atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Mama reading Axel a bedtime story in his cozy room

pages.text: "I love you, Mama," yawned Axel. "I'm not scared anymore. I feel brave and happy." Soon he was fast asleep, dreaming sweet dreams under the gentle stars.

illustration.description: Axel peacefully sleeping in his bed with a gentle smile on his face. The star night light continues to cast soft patterns around the room. Through his window, diverse families in other houses can be seen with their own bedtime routines, creating a sense of community and comfort.
illustration.alt: Axel sleeping peacefully with happy dreams

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Axel feel about bedtime at first?
questions.explanation: Axel felt scared when Mama said it was bedtime. His tummy felt funny and his heart beat fast.

questions.question: What made Axel's room feel safer?
questions.explanation: The special night light that made star shapes on the wall helped make the scary shadows go away.

questions.question: What did Mama do to help Axel feel better?
choices: Gave him a hug and helped make bedtime cozy
questions.explanation: Mama hugged Axel and said it's okay to feel scared, then helped make bedtime feel safe and cozy.

questions.question: How did Axel feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Axel said 'I'm not scared anymore. I feel brave and happy' before falling asleep peacefully.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.text: Axel felt _____ when it was bedtime, but Mama's _____ made him feel better. The night light made _____ on the wall and helped Axel feel brave and _____.
